v16_real_teacher_cache_success.significance: real object-dense teacher cache is a genuine positive asset

progression.headline: naive multi-trace OSTF runs but is too weak
physics-prior residual clearly beats V17 but does not establish a CV win on the key M512 setting
key_points: M128 shows partial positive signal but M512 does not close the story

progression.headline: semantic fairness is fixed, but M128/M512 still do not beat CV
context-aware deterministic residuals still fail on all-average and CV-hard subsets
oracle multimodal diagnostics are positive, but only under best-of-K/minFDE evaluation
key_points: best variant v21_multimodal_m128_seed42_h8 beats CV on all-average minFDE by about +8.9 px and hard-subset minFDE by about +40.0 px

progression.headline: calibration makes the deployable top-1 path choose the CV mode
key_points: top1_mode_accuracy=1.0 against the oracle best mode because the oracle best mode is the CV mode
top1_non_cv_rate=0.0 on the best V22 runs
top1 point/endpoint remain worse than the CV baseline

v22_deployable_top1_vs_cv.interpretation: the deployable calibrated top-1 path falls back to the CV solution rather than producing a new object-dense forecasting advantage

why_current_setup_is_cv_saturated: The current CoTracker-teacher H8/H16 pseudo-targets are strongly explained by constant-velocity motion on most eligible clips.
V20 hard-subset auditing already showed CV saturation and only limited dense-point value on diagnostic subsets.
V21 gains appear only under oracle best-of-K / minFDE style evaluation, not under the deterministic top-1 path required for a usable deployed model.
V22 calibration improves mode selection, but the calibrated selector chooses the CV mode rather than a learned non-CV dense-motion mode.
The strongest current signal is therefore that the setup is teacher/benchmark saturated, not that object-dense modeling is solved.
why_object_dense_semantic_trace_field_cl: Current deployable top-1 mode does not beat CV in the live V22 setup.
Dense-point and semantic-coupling evidence is not robust enough to support a main claim.
Oracle best-of-K gains do not by themselves justify a usable world-model claim.
The current benchmark/teacher combination does not force the model to use non-CV object-dense structure in a claimable way.
why_continuing_v23_small_model_trials_is: The bottleneck is no longer just local decoder capacity; the calibrated top-1 solution already selects the CV mode.
Continuing to train more small H8/H16 variants in the same setup is likely to keep optimizing within a CV-saturated teacher/benchmark regime.
The more promising next step is a redesign of the teacher/benchmark stack, not another same-setup trial.
ostf_allowed_appendix_claims: Real CoTracker-official object-dense teacher caches are feasible and validated in the live repo.
OSTF V17-V22 provides a useful negative/diagnostic progression from naive multi-trace to physics-prior and multimodal variants.
Oracle best-of-K/minFDE diagnostics show that multimodal object-dense hypotheses can contain useful alternatives on hard subsets.
The current CoTracker-teacher H8/H16 setup remains CV-dominated at deployable top-1 mode selection.
ostf_forbidden_main_claims: OSTF beats CV in the current setup.
Object-dense semantic trace field world model is solved or established.
Dense field should become a main-paper claim.
V21 oracle best-of-K should be presented as a deployable top-1 world-model result.
